sections.paragraph:
ὑπέρτατος, η, ον (Hom. et al.; SEG XLII, 846 p. 245; PStras 40, 41) superl. of ὑπέρ pert. to the highest point on a scale of extent, uppermost, loftiest, supreme (Aeschyl., Suppl. 672 of Zeus) God’s ὑπερτάτη ὄψις 1 Cl 36:2. τῇ ὑπερτάτῃ (v.l.-τάτῳ adj. of two terminations) αὐτοῦ (i.e. God’s) βουλήσει 40:3.—DELG s.v. ὑπέρ.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

PStras PStras = Griechische Papyrus der kaiserlichen Universitäts- und Landesbibliothek zu Strassburg—List 4

superl. superl. = superlative

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

adj. adj. = adjective

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1034
